subUnits.title: 9.1 Calculation of Local Time Using Longitudes
notes: • Earth rotates 360° in 24 hours → 15° per hour → 1° per 4 minutes
• Local time is the actual solar time at a specific longitude
• Places east of a reference point have earlier (faster) time; places west have later (slower) time
1. Find the difference in longitude between two places
2. Convert difference to time: Multiply by 4 minutes (or divide by 15 for hours)
Question: If it is 12:00 noon at Greenwich (0°), what is the local time at Lagos (3°E)?
• Lagos is East of Greenwich → ADD
Question: If it is 6:00 pm at 30°E, what is the time at 15°W?
• Forgetting to add longitudes when in different hemispheres
• Adding when moving West or subtracting when moving East
diagramDescriptions: World map showing longitudinal time zones with 15° intervals marked
Step-by-step calculation flowchart: Longitude diff → ×4 min → Apply East/West rule → Final time
Diagram illustrating Earth's rotation direction and time progression from East to West
Comparison table: Adding vs Subtracting time with clear examples

quiz.q: Earth rotates 360° in 24 hours, so 1° of longitude equals:
quiz.explanation: 24 hours = 1440 minutes ÷ 360° = 4 minutes per degree of longitude.

quiz.q: When calculating time, moving East from a reference longitude means you:
quiz.explanation: Earth rotates West to East, so places further East experience sunrise earlier and have faster time.

quiz.q: If it is 10:00 am at 0° (GMT), the time at 15°E is:
Two places at 20°W and 10°E have a longitude difference of:
quiz.explanation: Different hemispheres: add the degrees. 20° + 10° = 30° difference.

quiz.q: If it is 2:00 pm at 45°E, the time at 30°W is:
quiz.explanation: Diff = 45+30=75° → 75×4=300 min = 5 hrs. 30°W is West → subtract: 2:00 pm - 5:00 = 9:00 am? Wait, 14:00 - 5:00 = 9:00 am. Let me recalculate: 14:00 - 5:00 = 09:00. I'll adjust option A to 9:00 am for accuracy, but to keep format clean I'll fix the explanation to match 9:00 am. Actually, 2pm = 14:00. 14-5=9. I'll update the correct option to 9:00 am. Let's keep it consistent: opts: ['7:00 am', '8:00 am', '9:00 am', '11:00 am'], ans: 2.

quiz.q: A place at 60°W experiences sunrise ______ than a place at 30°W.
quiz.explanation: 60°W is further West, so Earth reaches 30°W first. Sunrise occurs later at 60°W.

quiz.q: To convert 135 minutes to hours, you divide by:
quiz.explanation: 1 hour = 60 minutes. 135 ÷ 60 = 2 hours 15 minutes.

quiz.q: If City A is at 10°E (time 8:00 am) and City B is at 5°E, City B's time is:
quiz.explanation: Diff = 5° → 20 min. B is West of A → subtract: 8:00 - 0:20 = 7:40 am.

quiz.q: Local time differs from standard time because local time is based on:
quiz.explanation: Local solar time is calculated directly from a location's exact longitude, while standard time follows political time zones.

quiz.q: When solving time problems, if the result exceeds 24:00 or goes below 00:00, you should:
opts: Add or subtract 24 hours and change the date
quiz.explanation: Crossing midnight requires adjusting the calendar date forward (if >24:00) or backward (if <00:00).

subUnits.title: 9.2 International Date Line, GMT & Time Zones
notes: • Basis for global timekeeping and navigation
• Also called Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) in modern systems
• Earth divided into 24 time zones, each ~15° longitude wide
• Each zone differs by 1 hour from adjacent zones
• Countries adopt standard time for administrative convenience
• Some countries span multiple zones (USA, Russia, Canada)
• Nigeria uses West Africa Time (WAT) = GMT+1
• Roughly follows 180° longitude through Pacific Ocean
• Marks where the calendar date changes
• Crossing eastward: subtract one day (lose a day)
• Crossing westward: add one day (gain a day)
• Zigzags to avoid splitting countries/islands (e.g., Kiribati, Fiji, Alaska)
• Works with time zones to maintain global date consistency
• Check if longitudes are in same or different hemispheres
• Remember IDL crossing rules: East = -1 day, West = +1 day
• Use 24-hour clock to avoid AM/PM confusion in calculations

Diagram illustrating date change when crossing IDL east vs west
Chart comparing GMT, UTC, and major world time zones
Flowchart for solving time zone problems with IDL considerations

quiz.q: Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is measured at:
quiz.explanation: GMT is the reference time at the Prime Meridian (0° longitude) passing through Greenwich, London.

quiz.q: The International Date Line is approximately located at:
quiz.explanation: The IDL roughly follows the 180° meridian in the Pacific Ocean, where the calendar date changes.

quiz.q: When crossing the International Date Line eastward, you:
quiz.explanation: Crossing IDL eastward means moving into an earlier time zone, so you subtract one day from the calendar.

quiz.q: Nigeria's standard time is:
quiz.explanation: Nigeria uses West Africa Time (WAT), which is GMT+1 (one hour ahead of Greenwich).

quiz.q: Time zones are approximately ______ degrees wide.
quiz.explanation: 360° ÷ 24 hours = 15° per time zone, each differing by 1 hour from its neighbors.

quiz.q: The International Date Line zigzags to:
quiz.explanation: The IDL deviates from 180° to prevent splitting nations like Kiribati, Fiji, and Russia into two different dates.

quiz.q: Standard time is preferred over local solar time because it:
opts: Provides uniformity for administration and transport
quiz.explanation: Standard time simplifies scheduling, communication, and governance by grouping regions into shared time zones.

quiz.q: If it is Monday 10:00 am just west of the IDL, the time just east of the IDL is:
quiz.explanation: Moving east across IDL subtracts a day. Same clock time, but calendar moves back to Sunday.

quiz.q: UTC stands for:
quiz.explanation: UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) is the modern international standard replacing GMT for precise global timekeeping.

quiz.q: A country spanning multiple time zones must:
opts: Adopt different standard times for different regions
quiz.explanation: Large countries like USA, Russia, and Australia use multiple time zones to align local time with solar time across regions.
